papers.title: MDCompress: better, faster compression of molecular dynamics simulation trajectories
papers.summary: Abstract Motivation Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations model the physical movements of atoms in biomolecular systems over time, providing atomic-resolution insight into conformational changes, binding events, and dynamic behaviors that cannot be captured by static structures alone. As such, MD simulations are playing an increasingly important role in understanding the functional roles and molecular interactions of proteins. However, trajectories from these simulations can be extremely large, often reaching tens of gigabytes for a single simulation of modest duration. This creates substantial challenges for storage and data transfer, motivating efficient compression strategies. Furthermore, many downstream analyses require extraction of only a subset of frames or specific atoms from the full trajectory, so an ideal compression format should support rapid random-access decompression of such samplings without requiring full file decompression. Results Here, we introduce MDCompress, a new trajectory compression format and accompanying software implementation that meets these goals. MDCompress produces compressed trajectory files that are 15–37% smaller than those generated by the widely-used XTC format, while achieving faster compression and decompression speeds through efficient multithreading. Availability and Implementation The MDCompress software and library are released under an open license (BSD-3) and may be downloaded at https://github.com/refresh-bio/mdcompress and is also available as a Zenodo repository at 10.5281/zenodo.19218347
papers.llm_title_zh: MDCompress：更优、更快的分子动力学模拟轨迹压缩
papers.llm_title_bilingual: MDCompress：更优、更快的分子动力学模拟轨迹压缩 / MDCompress: better, faster compression of molecular dynamics simulation trajectories
papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何为分子动力学模拟轨迹提供一种兼具更高压缩率、更快压缩/解压速度，并支持对子集帧或特定原子进行快速随机访问解压的压缩格式与实现？
llm_highlights_zh: MDCompress是一种面向分子动力学轨迹的新型压缩格式及软件实现。
与广泛使用的XTC格式相比，其压缩文件可缩小15%–37%，且压缩与解压速度更快。
该方法支持高效多线程处理，旨在兼顾大规模轨迹存储、传输与随机访问解压需求。
papers.llm_rss_summary_zh:
研究动机：分子动力学（MD）模拟能够刻画生物分子体系中原子的时间演化过程，为构象变化、结合事件及动态行为提供原子分辨率信息，这些信息无法仅凭静态结构获得。因此，MD模拟在理解蛋白质功能及其分子相互作用方面日益重要。然而，这类模拟产生的轨迹数据体量通常极大，即便是时长适中的单次模拟，也常可达到数十GB，给数据存储与传输带来显著挑战，因而亟需高效压缩策略。此外，许多下游分析仅需提取完整轨迹中的部分帧或特定原子，因此理想的压缩格式还应支持对这些采样数据进行快速随机访问解压，而无需先对整个文件完全解压。

研究结果：本文提出了MDCompress，这是一种新的轨迹压缩格式及其配套软件实现，可满足上述需求。与广泛使用的XTC格式相比，MDCompress生成的压缩轨迹文件可再缩小15%–37%，并通过高效多线程实现更快的压缩与解压速度。

可用性与实现：MDCompress软件及程序库采用BSD-3开源许可证发布，可从https://github.com/refresh-bio/mdcompress 下载，也可通过Zenodo获取，DOI为10.5281/zenodo.19218347。

papers.title: Harvesting more reads from single-cell combinatorial barcoding data with scarecrow
papers.summary: Abstract Summary Combinatorial barcoding technologies for single-cell nucleotide sequencing, such as split-pool ligation protocols, involve sequential rounds of cell barcoding to uniquely tag individual cells. The rapid adoption of combinatorial barcoding in recent years is due in part to its scalability across cells and samples. However, small shifts in barcode positions within sequencing reads caused by technical artifacts, e.g. during barcode incorporation or synthesis, can impact the accurate assignment of reads to cell barcodes. Existing processing tools typically assume barcodes contain fixed-length nucleotide sequences located at fixed positions within reads, overlooking any positional variability. Consequently, reads containing truncated or mispositioned barcodes are discarded during initial data processing steps leading to significant data loss. To solve this limitation and maximise the retention of sequencing reads from single-cell combinatorial barcoding experiments, we introduce scarecrow . This tool screens a subsample of reads to generate position-specific barcode profiles, which are then used to flexibly identify barcode sequences in each read whilst accounting for positional errors, a phenomenon we refer to as “jitter”. Barcode matches are then prioritised to minimise nucleotide mismatches and the degree of jitter. These initial profiles are subsequently used to extract and error correct barcode combinations in high throughput sequencing libraries. By incorporating jitter into barcode error correction, scarecrow enables greater data recovery and improved downstream single-cell analyses. Scarecrow is fully open access, implemented in Python, and generates output files using standardised sequence file formats for maximal interoperability. A detailed explanation of the scarecrow workflow can be found in the supplementary materials. Availability Scarecrow is freely available on GitHub https://github.com/MorganResearchLab/scarecrow and Zenodo https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18621784
papers.llm_title_zh: 利用 scarecrow 从单细胞组合条形码数据中获取更多读段
papers.llm_title_bilingual: 利用 scarecrow 从单细胞组合条形码数据中获取更多读段 / Harvesting more reads from single-cell combinatorial barcoding data with scarecrow
papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何在单细胞组合条形码测序数据处理中识别并校正条形码位置偏移等技术伪差，从而减少因固定位置假设造成的读段丢失并提高下游分析质量？
llm_highlights_zh: scarecrow针对单细胞组合条形码测序中条形码位置偏移（jitter）导致的读段丢失问题提出解决方案。
该方法先基于读段子样本建立位置特异性条形码谱，再在允许位置误差的条件下识别并纠正条形码组合。
将jitter纳入条形码纠错后，可提高测序读段保留率，并改善后续单细胞分析表现。
papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 单细胞核酸测序中的组合条形码技术（如split-pool连接方案）通过连续多轮细胞条形码标记来唯一标识单个细胞，因其在细胞与样本层面的良好可扩展性而被广泛采用。然而，条形码掺入或合成过程中产生的技术伪差可导致测序读段内条形码位置发生轻微偏移，从而影响读段与细胞条形码的准确对应。现有处理工具通常假定条形码为位于读段固定位置的固定长度核苷酸序列，忽视了这种位置可变性。因此，含有截短或错位条形码的读段常在初始处理中被直接丢弃，造成显著数据损失。为解决这一局限并最大化保留单细胞组合条形码实验中的测序读段，作者提出了scarecrow。该工具先筛查部分读段以生成位置特异性条形码谱，随后在考虑位置误差（作者称为“jitter”）的前提下，灵活识别每条读段中的条形码序列；并通过优先最小化核苷酸错配数及jitter程度来确定条形码匹配结果。在此基础上，这些初始谱图还可用于高通量测序文库中条形码组合的提取与纠错。通过在条形码纠错过程中纳入jitter信息，scarecrow可提高数据回收率并改善后续单细胞分析。该工具采用Python实现、完全开放获取，并以标准化序列文件格式输出结果以增强互操作性。

papers.primary_summary_zh: 单细胞核酸测序中的组合条形码技术（如split-pool连接方案）通过连续多轮细胞条形码标记来唯一标识单个细胞，因其在细胞与样本层面的良好可扩展性而被广泛采用。然而，条形码掺入或合成过程中产生的技术伪差可导致测序读段内条形码位置发生轻微偏移，从而影响读段与细胞条形码的准确对应。现有处理工具通常假定条形码为位于读段固定位置的固定长度核苷酸序列，忽视了这种位置可变性。因此，含有截短或错位条形码的读段常在初始处理中被直接丢弃，造成显著数据损失。为解决这一局限并最大化保留单细胞组合条形码实验中的测序读段，作者提出了scarecrow。该工具先筛查部分读段以生成位置特异性条形码谱，随后在考虑位置误差（作者称为“jitter”）的前提下，灵活识别每条读段中的条形码序列；并通过优先最小化核苷酸错配数及jitter程度来确定条形码匹配结果。在此基础上，这些初始谱图还可用于高通量测序文库中条形码组合的提取与纠错。通过在条形码纠错过程中纳入jitter信息，scarecrow可提高数据回收率并改善后续单细胞分析。该工具采用Python实现、完全开放获取，并以标准化序列文件格式输出结果以增强互操作性。

papers.title: DrugBLIP: Exploring the Protein-Molecule Interaction Mechanisms with a Multi-task Learning Graph Transformer
papers.summary: Abstract Motivation Traditional drug discovery methods are costly and inefficient, while existing deep learning approaches remain limited by task specificity and practical applicability. Accurately modeling protein-molecule interactions is critical for advancing virtual screening, docking, and drug design. Results We propose DrugBLIP, a multi-task graph transformer model based on SE(3)-equivariant architectures, to unify protein-molecule interaction learning. By integrating contrastive learning, matching tasks, and docking optimization, DrugBLIP captures 3D spatial relationships through a hybrid graph transformer framework. Evaluations demonstrate state-of-the-art performance: DrugBLIP achieves an AUROC of 0.8217 and BEDROC of 0.5743 on virtual screening, outperforming traditional and deep learning baselines by 10–127% across metrics. It also attains 91.2% top-1 docking success on CASF-2016 and 41.8% target fishing accuracy, showcasing robustness in diverse scenarios. Additionally, DrugBLIP reduces computational time by 700× compared to traditional docking tools. Availability and implementation Code is available at https://github.com/Wolkenwandler/DrugBLIP and archived at Zenodo with

papers.llm_title_bilingual: DrugBLIP：利用多任务学习图Transformer探索蛋白质-小分子相互作用机制 / DrugBLIP: Exploring the Protein-Molecule Interaction Mechanisms with a Multi-task Learning Graph Transformer
papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何构建一种能够统一建模蛋白质-小分子相互作用的多任务深度学习框架，以同时提升虚拟筛选、分子对接和靶标识别等任务的性能与实用性？
llm_highlights_zh: 提出基于SE(3)等变架构的多任务图Transformer模型DrugBLIP，用于统一学习蛋白质-小分子相互作用。
该模型结合对比学习、匹配任务与对接优化，可表征三维空间关系，并在虚拟筛选、分子对接和靶标识别中表现稳健。
实验显示DrugBLIP在虚拟筛选与对接任务上达到先进水平，且较传统对接工具可实现约700倍计算加速。
papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 【摘要】研究动机：传统药物发现方法成本高、效率低，而现有深度学习方法常受限于特定任务，实际应用性不足。准确建模蛋白质-小分子相互作用对于推动虚拟筛选、分子对接和药物设计至关重要。研究结果：作者提出DrugBLIP，这是一种基于SE(3)等变架构的多任务图Transformer模型，用于统一学习蛋白质-小分子相互作用。通过整合对比学习、匹配任务和对接优化，DrugBLIP借助混合图Transformer框架捕获三维空间关系。评估结果表明，该方法达到当前先进水平：在虚拟筛选任务中，DrugBLIP取得0.8217的AUROC和0.5743的BEDROC，相较传统方法和深度学习基线在多项指标上提升10%–127%。在CASF-2016上，其分子对接top-1成功率达到91.2%，靶标识别准确率为41.8%，显示出在多种场景中的稳健性。此外，与传统对接工具相比，DrugBLIP可将计算时间降低约700倍。代码可在GitHub获取，并归档于Zenodo。

papers.title: DTH: A nonparametric test for homogeneity of multivariate dispersions
papers.summary: Abstract Motivation Testing for differences in within-group dispersion is a fundamental problem in multivariate data analysis, with direct implications for interpreting group structure and validating statistical assumptions of other analysis such as ANOVA. Existing methods typically construct test statistics either based on the distance of each observation from the group center or on the mean of pairwise dissimilarities among observations within a group. Both approaches can fail when the mean within-group distance is similar across groups but the distributions of the within-group distances differ. This issue is particularly relevant in high-dimensional microbiome data, where outliers and overdispersion can distort the performance of mean-dissimilarity-based tests. Results We introduce the non-parametric Distance-based Test for Homogeneity (DTH), which measures dispersion of a group by computing within-group dissimilarity. Difference in dispersion across groups is tested by comparing the distributions of the within-group dissimilarity across different groups. A combination of Kolmogorov-Smirnov and Wasserstein distances are used to construct the difference between the distributions. For more than two groups, pairwise group tests are combined using a permutation-based p-value. Through simulations, we show that our method has higher power than existing tests for homogeneity in certain situations and comparable power in others. For continuous covariates, we offer an heuristic extension of DTH that showed good performance in simulations. Availability and implementation The DTH package, along with the code for reproducing all simulations, analyses, and an accompanying vignette, is available at https://github.com/asmita112358/DTH
papers.llm_title_zh: DTH：一种用于多变量离散度同质性检验的非参数方法
papers.llm_title_bilingual: DTH：一种用于多变量离散度同质性检验的非参数方法 / DTH: A nonparametric test for homogeneity of multivariate dispersions
papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何在多变量数据中，尤其是在高维微生物组等易受离群值和过度离散影响的场景下，基于组内不相似度分布而非均值水平，更稳健地检验不同组之间离散度是否同质？
llm_highlights_zh: 提出一种用于检验多变量组内离散度同质性的非参数方法DTH。
该方法比较各组组内不相似度分布，并结合Kolmogorov-Smirnov距离与Wasserstein距离构建检验统计量。
模拟结果显示，DTH在部分情形下较现有同质性检验具有更高检验效能，在其他情形下表现相当。
papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 【摘要】研究动机：检验组内离散度差异是多变量数据分析中的基础问题，直接关系到组结构的解释以及ANOVA等其他分析方法统计假设的验证。现有方法通常基于每个观测值到组中心的距离，或基于组内观测两两不相似度的均值来构建检验统计量。当各组平均组内距离相近、但组内距离分布不同的时候，这两类方法都可能失效。该问题在高维微生物组数据中尤为突出，因为离群值和过度离散会扭曲基于平均不相似度的检验表现。研究结果：作者提出非参数的基于距离的同质性检验（Distance-based Test for Homogeneity，DTH），通过计算组内不相似度来度量组的离散程度，并通过比较不同组之间组内不相似度的分布来检验离散度差异。方法结合Kolmogorov-Smirnov距离与Wasserstein距离来刻画分布间差异。对于两组以上情形，采用基于置换的P值将两两组间检验进行整合。模拟结果表明，在某些情形下，该方法较现有同质性检验具有更高的统计功效，而在其他情形下功效相当。对于连续协变量，作者还提出了DTH的启发式扩展，并在模拟中显示出良好表现。可用性与实现：DTH软件包及复现全部模拟、分析和配套说明文档的代码可见：https://github.com/asmita112358/DTH

papers.title: CurrentView: A tool for visualization and comparison of nanopore ionic current signals
papers.summary: Abstract Summary Nanopore sequencing measures ionic current as native DNA or RNA molecules move through a biological pore. The resulting ionic current changes are inferred into sequence by Oxford Nanopore Technologies’ Dorado basecaller. These data permit direct analysis of nucleotide sequences and modifications. Dorado basecaller also outputs a move-table that contains approximate mappings between ionic current signal and basecalled sequence. This ionic current information can be visualized at specific positions given the alignment between read sequence and a reference sequence. We present CurrentView, a fast and user-friendly toolkit for reference-guided visualization of nanopore ionic current signals. CurrentView uses conventional sequence alignment and move-table information from BAM files, and signal information from ONT POD5 files to extract and visualize ionic current traces at specific positions. The toolkit supports simultaneous comparison across multiple experimental conditions, computes summary statistics through kernel density estimation and histograms, and enables visual analysis of signal patterns associated with modifications or sequence context. Notably, CurrentView can visualize and analyze more than two conditions at once. It supports UMAP dimensionality reduction and Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) clustering, enabling the identification of distinct signal populations across experimental groups. CurrentView is available as both a Python API and an exploratory interactive web application, allowing researchers to rapidly inspect ionic current patterns and compare conditions. Availability and Implementation CurrentView is fully open source and available on GitHub https://github.com/genometechlab/currentview . The repository includes full documentation, installation guide, usage instructions, and an example Jupyter notebook showing typical use cases similar to the one presented in the manuscript
papers.llm_title_zh: 纳米孔离子电流信号可视化与比较工具：CurrentView
papers.llm_title_bilingual: 纳米孔离子电流信号可视化与比较工具：CurrentView / CurrentView: A tool for visualization and comparison of nanopore ionic current signals
papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何基于参考序列对纳米孔测序离子电流信号进行高效可视化与多条件比较，并借助统计汇总和聚类分析识别与序列背景或修饰相关的信号模式？
llm_highlights_zh: CurrentView是一款用于纳米孔离子电流信号参考引导可视化与比较的快速、易用工具。
该工具整合BAM中的move-table与ONT POD5信号数据，可在特定位点提取并展示电流轨迹。
其支持多实验条件同时比较，并提供UMAP降维与GMM聚类以识别不同信号群体。
papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 纳米孔测序在天然DNA或RNA分子穿过生物孔道时测量离子电流，Oxford Nanopore Technologies 的Dorado碱基识别器将这些电流变化推断为序列。此类数据不仅可用于核苷酸序列与修饰的直接分析，Dorado还输出move-table，用于提供离子电流信号与碱基识别序列之间的近似对应关系。结合读取序列与参考序列的比对信息，即可在特定位点对离子电流信息进行可视化。本文提出CurrentView，这是一种快速且用户友好的工具包，用于参考引导的纳米孔离子电流信号可视化。CurrentView利用BAM文件中的常规序列比对与move-table信息，以及ONT POD5文件中的信号数据，在特定位点提取并可视化离子电流轨迹。该工具支持多个实验条件的同步比较，可通过核密度估计与直方图计算汇总统计量，并支持对与修饰或序列背景相关的信号模式进行可视分析。值得注意的是，CurrentView可同时可视化和分析两个以上条件。它还支持UMAP降维和高斯混合模型（GMM）聚类，从而识别不同实验组中的离散信号群体。CurrentView同时提供Python API和交互式网页应用，便于研究人员快速检查离子电流模式并比较不同条件。该工具为完全开源，代码托管于GitHub，并提供完整文档、安装指南、使用说明及示例Jupyter notebook。

The repository includes full documentation, installation guide, usage instructions, and an example Jupyter notebook showing typical use cases similar to the one presented in the manuscript
papers.primary_summary_zh: 纳米孔测序在天然DNA或RNA分子穿过生物孔道时测量离子电流，Oxford Nanopore Technologies 的Dorado碱基识别器将这些电流变化推断为序列。此类数据不仅可用于核苷酸序列与修饰的直接分析，Dorado还输出move-table，用于提供离子电流信号与碱基识别序列之间的近似对应关系。结合读取序列与参考序列的比对信息，即可在特定位点对离子电流信息进行可视化。本文提出CurrentView，这是一种快速且用户友好的工具包，用于参考引导的纳米孔离子电流信号可视化。CurrentView利用BAM文件中的常规序列比对与move-table信息，以及ONT POD5文件中的信号数据，在特定位点提取并可视化离子电流轨迹。该工具支持多个实验条件的同步比较，可通过核密度估计与直方图计算汇总统计量，并支持对与修饰或序列背景相关的信号模式进行可视分析。值得注意的是，CurrentView可同时可视化和分析两个以上条件。它还支持UMAP降维和高斯混合模型（GMM）聚类，从而识别不同实验组中的离散信号群体。CurrentView同时提供Python API和交互式网页应用，便于研究人员快速检查离子电流模式并比较不同条件。该工具为完全开源，代码托管于GitHub，并提供完整文档、安装指南、使用说明及示例Jupyter notebook。

papers.title: Accelerated long-read variant calling with Clair3 for whole-genome sequencing
papers.summary: Abstract Summary The rapid growth of genomic data and increasing adoption of long-read sequencing technologies have rendered variant calling one of the most computationally demanding tasks in genomic analysis. Although deep learning-based methods currently outperform conventional approaches in distinguishing true variants from complex sequencing noise, they impose prohibitive computational and time requirements. To address this limitation, we present a computational framework based on Clair3 that integrates parallelized feature generation, enhanced variant phasing, in-memory read haplotagging, and GPU-accelerated neural network inference to accelerate variant calling. By dynamically optimizing the use of both GPU and CPU resources, our method achieves substantial runtime improvements without compromising accuracy. We evaluated our framework across a range of sequencing depths, diverse samples, and multiple hardware configurations. Our results demonstrate that the optimized pipeline completes variant calling for a 30× whole-genome sequence in 12–20 minutes using standard computational resources (32 CPU threads and one NVIDIA GPU), and in 12–15 minutes on an Apple Mac Studio (32 threads), which is ∼10–20-fold speedup compared with its initial release. In addition to exceptional efficiency, our method maintains state-of-the-art accuracy, achieving SNP F1-scores of 99.32% and 99.70% on 30× ONT and PacBio GIAB HG003 datasets, respectively. This work introduces a rapid, accurate, and scalable variant calling framework that effectively supports large-cohort genomic studies and time-sensitive clinical applications. Availability and Implementation The accelerated implementation of Clair3 is open source and available at: https://github.com/HKU-BAL/Clair3/tree/gpu
papers.llm_title_zh: Clair3用于全基因组测序的长读长变异检测加速
papers.llm_title_bilingual: Clair3用于全基因组测序的长读长变异检测加速 / Accelerated long-read variant calling with Clair3 for whole-genome sequencing
papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何在保持长读长全基因组测序变异检测高准确性的同时，显著降低基于深度学习的Clair3方法的计算与时间成本，以满足大队列研究和时效性临床应用的需求？
llm_highlights_zh: 基于Clair3构建加速型长读长变异检测框架，整合并行特征生成、增强变异分相、内存内read haplotagging与GPU加速神经网络推理。
在不降低准确性的前提下，30×全基因组测序的变异检测可在常规计算资源下于12–20分钟完成，在Apple Mac Studio上可于12–15分钟完成。
该方法较Clair3初始版本实现约10–20倍加速，并在30× ONT与PacBio GIAB HG003数据集上分别达到99.32%和99.70%的SNP F1分数。
papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 随着基因组数据快速增长以及长读长测序技术的广泛应用，变异检测已成为基因组分析中计算负担最重的任务之一。尽管基于深度学习的方法在区分真实变异与复杂测序噪声方面优于传统方法，但其计算与时间开销往往过高。为解决这一问题，本文提出一种基于Clair3的计算框架，整合并行化特征生成、改进的变异分相、内存内read haplotagging以及GPU加速的神经网络推理，并通过动态优化GPU与CPU资源利用实现显著提速且不牺牲准确性。作者在不同测序深度、多种样本及多类硬件配置下对该框架进行了评估。结果显示，对于30×全基因组测序数据，该优化流程在标准计算资源（32个CPU线程和1块NVIDIA GPU）下可于12–20分钟内完成变异检测，在Apple Mac Studio（32线程）上可于12–15分钟内完成，较其初始版本加速约10–20倍。同时，该方法保持了当前先进水平的准确性，在30× ONT和PacBio GIAB HG003数据集中，SNP F1分数分别达到99.32%和99.70%。该研究提出了一种快速、准确且可扩展的变异检测框架，可有效支持大规模队列基因组研究及时间敏感的临床应用。开源实现见：https://github.com/HKU-BAL/Clair3/tree/gpu

all_papers.title: MDCompress: better, faster compression of molecular dynamics simulation trajectories
all_papers.summary: Abstract Motivation Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations model the physical movements of atoms in biomolecular systems over time, providing atomic-resolution insight into conformational changes, binding events, and dynamic behaviors that cannot be captured by static structures alone. As such, MD simulations are playing an increasingly important role in understanding the functional roles and molecular interactions of proteins. However, trajectories from these simulations can be extremely large, often reaching tens of gigabytes for a single simulation of modest duration. This creates substantial challenges for storage and data transfer, motivating efficient compression strategies. Furthermore, many downstream analyses require extraction of only a subset of frames or specific atoms from the full trajectory, so an ideal compression format should support rapid random-access decompression of such samplings without requiring full file decompression. Results Here, we introduce MDCompress, a new trajectory compression format and accompanying software implementation that meets these goals. MDCompress produces compressed trajectory files that are 15–37% smaller than those generated by the widely-used XTC format, while achieving faster compression and decompression speeds through efficient multithreading. Availability and Implementation The MDCompress software and library are released under an open license (BSD-3) and may be downloaded at https://github.com/refresh-bio/mdcompress and is also available as a Zenodo repository at 10.5281/zenodo.19218347

all_papers.llm_rss_summary_zh:
研究动机：分子动力学（MD）模拟能够刻画生物分子体系中原子的时间演化过程，为构象变化、结合事件及动态行为提供原子分辨率信息，这些信息无法仅凭静态结构获得。因此，MD模拟在理解蛋白质功能及其分子相互作用方面日益重要。然而，这类模拟产生的轨迹数据体量通常极大，即便是时长适中的单次模拟，也常可达到数十GB，给数据存储与传输带来显著挑战，因而亟需高效压缩策略。此外，许多下游分析仅需提取完整轨迹中的部分帧或特定原子，因此理想的压缩格式还应支持对这些采样数据进行快速随机访问解压，而无需先对整个文件完全解压。

研究结果：本文提出了MDCompress，这是一种新的轨迹压缩格式及其配套软件实现，可满足上述需求。与广泛使用的XTC格式相比，MDCompress生成的压缩轨迹文件可再缩小15%–37%，并通过高效多线程实现更快的压缩与解压速度。

可用性与实现：MDCompress软件及程序库采用BSD-3开源许可证发布，可从https://github.com/refresh-bio/mdcompress 下载，也可通过Zenodo获取，DOI为10.5281/zenodo.19218347。

all_papers.primary_summary_raw: Abstract Motivation Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations model the physical movements of atoms in biomolecular systems over time, providing atomic-resolution insight into conformational changes, binding events, and dynamic behaviors that cannot be captured by static structures alone. As such, MD simulations are playing an increasingly important role in understanding the functional roles and molecular interactions of proteins. However, trajectories from these simulations can be extremely large, often reaching tens of gigabytes for a single simulation of modest duration. This creates substantial challenges for storage and data transfer, motivating efficient compression strategies. Furthermore, many downstream analyses require extraction of only a subset of frames or specific atoms from the full trajectory, so an ideal compression format should support rapid random-access decompression of such samplings without requiring full file decompression. Results Here, we introduce MDCompress, a new trajectory compression format and accompanying software implementation that meets these goals. MDCompress produces compressed trajectory files that are 15–37% smaller than those generated by the widely-used XTC format, while achieving faster compression and decompression speeds through efficient multithreading. Availability and Implementation The MDCompress software and library are released under an open license (BSD-3) and may be downloaded at https://github.com/refresh-bio/mdcompress and is also available as a Zenodo repository at 10.5281/zenodo.19218347

all_papers.title: Harvesting more reads from single-cell combinatorial barcoding data with scarecrow
all_papers.summary: Abstract Summary Combinatorial barcoding technologies for single-cell nucleotide sequencing, such as split-pool ligation protocols, involve sequential rounds of cell barcoding to uniquely tag individual cells. The rapid adoption of combinatorial barcoding in recent years is due in part to its scalability across cells and samples. However, small shifts in barcode positions within sequencing reads caused by technical artifacts, e.g. during barcode incorporation or synthesis, can impact the accurate assignment of reads to cell barcodes. Existing processing tools typically assume barcodes contain fixed-length nucleotide sequences located at fixed positions within reads, overlooking any positional variability. Consequently, reads containing truncated or mispositioned barcodes are discarded during initial data processing steps leading to significant data loss. To solve this limitation and maximise the retention of sequencing reads from single-cell combinatorial barcoding experiments, we introduce scarecrow . This tool screens a subsample of reads to generate position-specific barcode profiles, which are then used to flexibly identify barcode sequences in each read whilst accounting for positional errors, a phenomenon we refer to as “jitter”. Barcode matches are then prioritised to minimise nucleotide mismatches and the degree of jitter. These initial profiles are subsequently used to extract and error correct barcode combinations in high throughput sequencing libraries. By incorporating jitter into barcode error correction, scarecrow enables greater data recovery and improved downstream single-cell analyses. Scarecrow is fully open access, implemented in Python, and generates output files using standardised sequence file formats for maximal interoperability. A detailed explanation of the scarecrow workflow can be found in the supplementary materials. Availability Scarecrow is freely available on GitHub https://github.com/MorganResearchLab/scarecrow and Zenodo https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18621784
all_papers.llm_title_zh: 利用 scarecrow 从单细胞组合条形码数据中获取更多读段
all_papers.llm_title_bilingual: 利用 scarecrow 从单细胞组合条形码数据中获取更多读段 / Harvesting more reads from single-cell combinatorial barcoding data with scarecrow
all_papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何在单细胞组合条形码测序数据处理中识别并校正条形码位置偏移等技术伪差，从而减少因固定位置假设造成的读段丢失并提高下游分析质量？
llm_highlights_zh: scarecrow针对单细胞组合条形码测序中条形码位置偏移（jitter）导致的读段丢失问题提出解决方案。
该方法先基于读段子样本建立位置特异性条形码谱，再在允许位置误差的条件下识别并纠正条形码组合。
将jitter纳入条形码纠错后，可提高测序读段保留率，并改善后续单细胞分析表现。
all_papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 单细胞核酸测序中的组合条形码技术（如split-pool连接方案）通过连续多轮细胞条形码标记来唯一标识单个细胞，因其在细胞与样本层面的良好可扩展性而被广泛采用。然而，条形码掺入或合成过程中产生的技术伪差可导致测序读段内条形码位置发生轻微偏移，从而影响读段与细胞条形码的准确对应。现有处理工具通常假定条形码为位于读段固定位置的固定长度核苷酸序列，忽视了这种位置可变性。因此，含有截短或错位条形码的读段常在初始处理中被直接丢弃，造成显著数据损失。为解决这一局限并最大化保留单细胞组合条形码实验中的测序读段，作者提出了scarecrow。该工具先筛查部分读段以生成位置特异性条形码谱，随后在考虑位置误差（作者称为“jitter”）的前提下，灵活识别每条读段中的条形码序列；并通过优先最小化核苷酸错配数及jitter程度来确定条形码匹配结果。在此基础上，这些初始谱图还可用于高通量测序文库中条形码组合的提取与纠错。通过在条形码纠错过程中纳入jitter信息，scarecrow可提高数据回收率并改善后续单细胞分析。该工具采用Python实现、完全开放获取，并以标准化序列文件格式输出结果以增强互操作性。

all_papers.title: DrugBLIP: Exploring the Protein-Molecule Interaction Mechanisms with a Multi-task Learning Graph Transformer
all_papers.summary: Abstract Motivation Traditional drug discovery methods are costly and inefficient, while existing deep learning approaches remain limited by task specificity and practical applicability. Accurately modeling protein-molecule interactions is critical for advancing virtual screening, docking, and drug design. Results We propose DrugBLIP, a multi-task graph transformer model based on SE(3)-equivariant architectures, to unify protein-molecule interaction learning. By integrating contrastive learning, matching tasks, and docking optimization, DrugBLIP captures 3D spatial relationships through a hybrid graph transformer framework. Evaluations demonstrate state-of-the-art performance: DrugBLIP achieves an AUROC of 0.8217 and BEDROC of 0.5743 on virtual screening, outperforming traditional and deep learning baselines by 10–127% across metrics. It also attains 91.2% top-1 docking success on CASF-2016 and 41.8% target fishing accuracy, showcasing robustness in diverse scenarios. Additionally, DrugBLIP reduces computational time by 700× compared to traditional docking tools. Availability and implementation Code is available at https://github.com/Wolkenwandler/DrugBLIP and archived at Zenodo with
all_papers.llm_title_zh: DrugBLIP：利用多任务学习图Transformer探索蛋白质-小分子相互作用机制
all_papers.llm_title_bilingual: DrugBLIP：利用多任务学习图Transformer探索蛋白质-小分子相互作用机制 / DrugBLIP: Exploring the Protein-Molecule Interaction Mechanisms with a Multi-task Learning Graph Transformer
all_papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何构建一种能够统一建模蛋白质-小分子相互作用的多任务深度学习框架，以同时提升虚拟筛选、分子对接和靶标识别等任务的性能与实用性？
llm_highlights_zh: 提出基于SE(3)等变架构的多任务图Transformer模型DrugBLIP，用于统一学习蛋白质-小分子相互作用。
该模型结合对比学习、匹配任务与对接优化，可表征三维空间关系，并在虚拟筛选、分子对接和靶标识别中表现稳健。
实验显示DrugBLIP在虚拟筛选与对接任务上达到先进水平，且较传统对接工具可实现约700倍计算加速。
all_papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 【摘要】研究动机：传统药物发现方法成本高、效率低，而现有深度学习方法常受限于特定任务，实际应用性不足。准确建模蛋白质-小分子相互作用对于推动虚拟筛选、分子对接和药物设计至关重要。研究结果：作者提出DrugBLIP，这是一种基于SE(3)等变架构的多任务图Transformer模型，用于统一学习蛋白质-小分子相互作用。通过整合对比学习、匹配任务和对接优化，DrugBLIP借助混合图Transformer框架捕获三维空间关系。评估结果表明，该方法达到当前先进水平：在虚拟筛选任务中，DrugBLIP取得0.8217的AUROC和0.5743的BEDROC，相较传统方法和深度学习基线在多项指标上提升10%–127%。在CASF-2016上，其分子对接top-1成功率达到91.2%，靶标识别准确率为41.8%，显示出在多种场景中的稳健性。此外，与传统对接工具相比，DrugBLIP可将计算时间降低约700倍。代码可在GitHub获取，并归档于Zenodo。

all_papers.title: DTH: A nonparametric test for homogeneity of multivariate dispersions
all_papers.summary: Abstract Motivation Testing for differences in within-group dispersion is a fundamental problem in multivariate data analysis, with direct implications for interpreting group structure and validating statistical assumptions of other analysis such as ANOVA. Existing methods typically construct test statistics either based on the distance of each observation from the group center or on the mean of pairwise dissimilarities among observations within a group. Both approaches can fail when the mean within-group distance is similar across groups but the distributions of the within-group distances differ. This issue is particularly relevant in high-dimensional microbiome data, where outliers and overdispersion can distort the performance of mean-dissimilarity-based tests. Results We introduce the non-parametric Distance-based Test for Homogeneity (DTH), which measures dispersion of a group by computing within-group dissimilarity. Difference in dispersion across groups is tested by comparing the distributions of the within-group dissimilarity across different groups. A combination of Kolmogorov-Smirnov and Wasserstein distances are used to construct the difference between the distributions. For more than two groups, pairwise group tests are combined using a permutation-based p-value. Through simulations, we show that our method has higher power than existing tests for homogeneity in certain situations and comparable power in others. all_papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何在多变量数据中，尤其是在高维微生物组等易受离群值和过度离散影响的场景下，基于组内不相似度分布而非均值水平，更稳健地检验不同组之间离散度是否同质？
llm_highlights_zh: 提出一种用于检验多变量组内离散度同质性的非参数方法DTH。
该方法比较各组组内不相似度分布，并结合Kolmogorov-Smirnov距离与Wasserstein距离构建检验统计量。
模拟结果显示，DTH在部分情形下较现有同质性检验具有更高检验效能，在其他情形下表现相当。
all_papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 【摘要】研究动机：检验组内离散度差异是多变量数据分析中的基础问题，直接关系到组结构的解释以及ANOVA等其他分析方法统计假设的验证。现有方法通常基于每个观测值到组中心的距离，或基于组内观测两两不相似度的均值来构建检验统计量。当各组平均组内距离相近、但组内距离分布不同的时候，这两类方法都可能失效。该问题在高维微生物组数据中尤为突出，因为离群值和过度离散会扭曲基于平均不相似度的检验表现。研究结果：作者提出非参数的基于距离的同质性检验（Distance-based Test for Homogeneity，DTH），通过计算组内不相似度来度量组的离散程度，并通过比较不同组之间组内不相似度的分布来检验离散度差异。方法结合Kolmogorov-Smirnov距离与Wasserstein距离来刻画分布间差异。对于两组以上情形，采用基于置换的P值将两两组间检验进行整合。模拟结果表明，在某些情形下，该方法较现有同质性检验具有更高的统计功效，而在其他情形下功效相当。对于连续协变量，作者还提出了DTH的启发式扩展，并在模拟中显示出良好表现。可用性与实现：DTH软件包及复现全部模拟、分析和配套说明文档的代码可见：https://github.com/asmita112358/DTH

all_papers.primary_summary_raw: Abstract Motivation Testing for differences in within-group dispersion is a fundamental problem in multivariate data analysis, with direct implications for interpreting group structure and validating statistical assumptions of other analysis such as ANOVA. Existing methods typically construct test statistics either based on the distance of each observation from the group center or on the mean of pairwise dissimilarities among observations within a group. Both approaches can fail when the mean within-group distance is similar across groups but the distributions of the within-group distances differ. This issue is particularly relevant in high-dimensional microbiome data, where outliers and overdispersion can distort the performance of mean-dissimilarity-based tests. Results We introduce the non-parametric Distance-based Test for Homogeneity (DTH), which measures dispersion of a group by computing within-group dissimilarity. Difference in dispersion across groups is tested by comparing the distributions of the within-group dissimilarity across different groups. A combination of Kolmogorov-Smirnov and Wasserstein distances are used to construct the difference between the distributions. For more than two groups, pairwise group tests are combined using a permutation-based p-value. Through simulations, we show that our method has higher power than existing tests for homogeneity in certain situations and comparable power in others. For continuous covariates, we offer an heuristic extension of DTH that showed good performance in simulations. Availability and implementation The DTH package, along with the code for reproducing all simulations, analyses, and an accompanying vignette, is available at https://github.com/asmita112358/DTH

all_papers.title: CurrentView: A tool for visualization and comparison of nanopore ionic current signals
all_papers.summary: Abstract Summary Nanopore sequencing measures ionic current as native DNA or RNA molecules move through a biological pore. The resulting ionic current changes are inferred into sequence by Oxford Nanopore Technologies’ Dorado basecaller. These data permit direct analysis of nucleotide sequences and modifications. Dorado basecaller also outputs a move-table that contains approximate mappings between ionic current signal and basecalled sequence. This ionic current information can be visualized at specific positions given the alignment between read sequence and a reference sequence. We present CurrentView, a fast and user-friendly toolkit for reference-guided visualization of nanopore ionic current signals. CurrentView uses conventional sequence alignment and move-table information from BAM files, and signal information from ONT POD5 files to extract and visualize ionic current traces at specific positions. The toolkit supports simultaneous comparison across multiple experimental conditions, computes summary statistics through kernel density estimation and histograms, and enables visual analysis of signal patterns associated with modifications or sequence context. Notably, CurrentView can visualize and analyze more than two conditions at once. It supports UMAP dimensionality reduction and Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM) clustering, enabling the identification of distinct signal populations across experimental groups. CurrentView is available as both a Python API and an exploratory interactive web application, allowing researchers to rapidly inspect ionic current patterns and compare conditions. Availability and Implementation CurrentView is fully open source and available on GitHub https://github.com/genometechlab/currentview . The repository includes full documentation, installation guide, usage instructions, and an example Jupyter notebook showing typical use cases similar to the one presented in the manuscript
all_papers.llm_title_zh: 纳米孔离子电流信号可视化与比较工具：CurrentView
all_papers.llm_title_bilingual: 纳米孔离子电流信号可视化与比较工具：CurrentView / CurrentView: A tool for visualization and comparison of nanopore ionic current signals
all_papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何基于参考序列对纳米孔测序离子电流信号进行高效可视化与多条件比较，并借助统计汇总和聚类分析识别与序列背景或修饰相关的信号模式？
llm_highlights_zh: CurrentView是一款用于纳米孔离子电流信号参考引导可视化与比较的快速、易用工具。
该工具整合BAM中的move-table与ONT POD5信号数据，可在特定位点提取并展示电流轨迹。
其支持多实验条件同时比较，并提供UMAP降维与GMM聚类以识别不同信号群体。
all_papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 纳米孔测序在天然DNA或RNA分子穿过生物孔道时测量离子电流，Oxford Nanopore Technologies 的Dorado碱基识别器将这些电流变化推断为序列。此类数据不仅可用于核苷酸序列与修饰的直接分析，Dorado还输出move-table，用于提供离子电流信号与碱基识别序列之间的近似对应关系。结合读取序列与参考序列的比对信息，即可在特定位点对离子电流信息进行可视化。本文提出CurrentView，这是一种快速且用户友好的工具包，用于参考引导的纳米孔离子电流信号可视化。CurrentView利用BAM文件中的常规序列比对与move-table信息，以及ONT POD5文件中的信号数据，在特定位点提取并可视化离子电流轨迹。该工具支持多个实验条件的同步比较，可通过核密度估计与直方图计算汇总统计量，并支持对与修饰或序列背景相关的信号模式进行可视分析。值得注意的是，CurrentView可同时可视化和分析两个以上条件。它还支持UMAP降维和高斯混合模型（GMM）聚类，从而识别不同实验组中的离散信号群体。CurrentView同时提供Python API和交互式网页应用，便于研究人员快速检查离子电流模式并比较不同条件。该工具为完全开源，代码托管于GitHub，并提供完整文档、安装指南、使用说明及示例Jupyter notebook。

all_papers.title: Accelerated long-read variant calling with Clair3 for whole-genome sequencing
all_papers.summary: Abstract Summary The rapid growth of genomic data and increasing adoption of long-read sequencing technologies have rendered variant calling one of the most computationally demanding tasks in genomic analysis. Although deep learning-based methods currently outperform conventional approaches in distinguishing true variants from complex sequencing noise, they impose prohibitive computational and time requirements. To address this limitation, we present a computational framework based on Clair3 that integrates parallelized feature generation, enhanced variant phasing, in-memory read haplotagging, and GPU-accelerated neural network inference to accelerate variant calling. By dynamically optimizing the use of both GPU and CPU resources, our method achieves substantial runtime improvements without compromising accuracy. We evaluated our framework across a range of sequencing depths, diverse samples, and multiple hardware configurations. Our results demonstrate that the optimized pipeline completes variant calling for a 30× whole-genome sequence in 12–20 minutes using standard computational resources (32 CPU threads and one NVIDIA GPU), and in 12–15 minutes on an Apple Mac Studio (32 threads), which is ∼10–20-fold speedup compared with its initial release. In addition to exceptional efficiency, our method maintains state-of-the-art accuracy, achieving SNP F1-scores of 99.32% and 99.70% on 30× ONT and PacBio GIAB HG003 datasets, respectively. This work introduces a rapid, accurate, and scalable variant calling framework that effectively supports large-cohort genomic studies and time-sensitive clinical applications. Availability and Implementation The accelerated implementation of Clair3 is open source and available at: https://github.com/HKU-BAL/Clair3/tree/gpu
all_papers.llm_title_zh: Clair3用于全基因组测序的长读长变异检测加速
all_papers.llm_title_bilingual: Clair3用于全基因组测序的长读长变异检测加速 / Accelerated long-read variant calling with Clair3 for whole-genome sequencing
all_papers.llm_research_question_zh: 如何在保持长读长全基因组测序变异检测高准确性的同时，显著降低基于深度学习的Clair3方法的计算与时间成本，以满足大队列研究和时效性临床应用的需求？
llm_highlights_zh: 基于Clair3构建加速型长读长变异检测框架，整合并行特征生成、增强变异分相、内存内read haplotagging与GPU加速神经网络推理。
在不降低准确性的前提下，30×全基因组测序的变异检测可在常规计算资源下于12–20分钟完成，在Apple Mac Studio上可于12–15分钟完成。
该方法较Clair3初始版本实现约10–20倍加速，并在30× ONT与PacBio GIAB HG003数据集上分别达到99.32%和99.70%的SNP F1分数。
all_papers.llm_rss_summary_zh: 随着基因组数据快速增长以及长读长测序技术的广泛应用，变异检测已成为基因组分析中计算负担最重的任务之一。尽管基于深度学习的方法在区分真实变异与复杂测序噪声方面优于传统方法，但其计算与时间开销往往过高。为解决这一问题，本文提出一种基于Clair3的计算框架，整合并行化特征生成、改进的变异分相、内存内read haplotagging以及GPU加速的神经网络推理，并通过动态优化GPU与CPU资源利用实现显著提速且不牺牲准确性。作者在不同测序深度、多种样本及多类硬件配置下对该框架进行了评估。结果显示，对于30×全基因组测序数据，该优化流程在标准计算资源（32个CPU线程和1块NVIDIA GPU）下可于12–20分钟内完成变异检测，在Apple Mac Studio（32线程）上可于12–15分钟内完成，较其初始版本加速约10–20倍。同时，该方法保持了当前先进水平的准确性，在30× ONT和PacBio GIAB HG003数据集中，SNP F1分数分别达到99.32%和99.70%。该研究提出了一种快速、准确且可扩展的变异检测框架，可有效支持大规模队列基因组研究及时间敏感的临床应用。开源实现见：https://github.com/HKU-BAL/Clair3/tree/gpu

all_papers.primary_summary_zh: 随着基因组数据快速增长以及长读长测序技术的广泛应用，变异检测已成为基因组分析中计算负担最重的任务之一。尽管基于深度学习的方法在区分真实变异与复杂测序噪声方面优于传统方法，但其计算与时间开销往往过高。为解决这一问题，本文提出一种基于Clair3的计算框架，整合并行化特征生成、改进的变异分相、内存内read haplotagging以及GPU加速的神经网络推理，并通过动态优化GPU与CPU资源利用实现显著提速且不牺牲准确性。作者在不同测序深度、多种样本及多类硬件配置下对该框架进行了评估。结果显示，对于30×全基因组测序数据，该优化流程在标准计算资源（32个CPU线程和1块NVIDIA GPU）下可于12–20分钟内完成变异检测，在Apple Mac Studio（32线程）上可于12–15分钟内完成，较其初始版本加速约10–20倍。同时，该方法保持了当前先进水平的准确性，在30× ONT和PacBio GIAB HG003数据集中，SNP F1分数分别达到99.32%和99.70%。该研究提出了一种快速、准确且可扩展的变异检测框架，可有效支持大规模队列基因组研究及时间敏感的临床应用。开源实现见：https://github.com/HKU-BAL/Clair3/tree/gpu
